title: alchemilla transiens var. corsica
intro: Alchemilla transiens var. corsica, also known as Corsican Lady's Mantle, is a variety of the alpine plant genus Alchemilla. It is characterized by its rosettes of rounded leaves and tiny white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and combat infections. Herbal preparations of Alchemilla transiens var. corsica, such as infusions, tinctures, and creams, can be used to treat a variety of health issues, including skin conditions, digestive problems, and menstrual cramps.
